summary: Jeff Atwood examines how Nextdoor, a neighborhood-focused social network, inadvertently became a hub for casual racism and racial profiling as it grew to include older, less progressive users. He argues that as social networks age beyond their young early-adopter base, they inevitably encounter more regressive social attitudes that correlate statistically with age. Rather than accepting this, Nextdoor redesigned their software with 'just in time nudges' that prompt users to focus on suspicious behavior rather than race when reporting incidents. This intervention reduced racial profiling posts by 75%, demonstrating that thoughtfully designed software can genuinely nudge people toward better behavior.
key_points: The smartphone revolution has brought the internet to populations who weren't traditional 'computer geeks,' fundamentally changing online communities
Social networks that start young inevitably age, and older users statistically hold more regressive social views—this is documented fact, not opinion
Nextdoor's reputation as a racial profiling hub emerged from the casual racism of well-connected neighbors
Software interventions at the right moment—'just in time nudges'—can measurably reduce problematic behavior (75% reduction in racial profiling posts)
The key design principle is focusing users on behavior rather than personal characteristics, applicable from Stack Overflow to parenting
money_quotes: The good news is that, at this moment, every human being is far more connected to their fellow humans than any human has ever been in the entirety of recorded history. Spoiler alert: that's also the bad news.
One unfortunate thing you'll find out when using Nextdoor is that your neighbors are probably a little bit racist.
The idea that regressive social opinions correlate with age isn't an opinion; it's a statistical fact.
I'm a huge fan of designing software to help nudge people, at exactly the right time, to be their better selves.
You never, ever judge someone by what they look like. Consider what they do instead.
For Nextdoor, quality is more important than quantity.
key_insight: Thoughtfully designed software interventions, delivered at exactly the right moment, can measurably reduce harmful behavior by nudging users to focus on actions rather than personal characteristics.
